records.ety: [F. buffet, LL. bufetum; of uncertain origin; perh. fr. the same source as E. buffet a blow, the root meaning to puff, hence (cf. puffed up) the idea of ostentation or display.]

defs: A cupboard or set of shelves, either movable or fixed at one side of a room, for the display of plate, china, etc., a sideboard.
quotes: Not when a gilt buffet's reflected pride Turns you from sound philosophy aside.

defs: A counter for food or refreshments.
A restaurant containing such a counter, as at a railroad station, or place of public gathering.

A meal set out on a buffet[2], arranged so that guests may serve themselves and choose those items that they desire; as, a buffet dinner. Diners usually take a plate provided and move in a line past the items on the buffet[2], placing those items they desire on the plate, to be eaten at some convenient place.

records.ety: [OE. buffet, boffet, OF. buffet a slap in the face, a pair of bellows, fr. buffe blow, cf. F. bouffer to blow, puff; prob. akin to E. puff. For the meaning slap, blow, cf. F. soufflet a slap, souffler to blow. See Puff, v. i., and cf. Buffet sidebroad, Buffoon]

defs: A blow with the hand; a slap on the face; a cuff.
quotes: When on his cheek a buffet fell.

defs: A blow from any source, or that which affects like a blow, as the violence of winds or waves; a stroke; an adverse action; an affliction; a trial; adversity.
quotes: Those planks of tough and hardy oak that used for yeas to brave the buffets of the Bay of Biscay.

defs: A small stool; a stool for a buffet or counter.
quotes: Go fetch us a light buffet.

records.ety: [OE. buffeten, OF. buffeter. See the preceding noun.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Buffeted; p. pr. & vb. n. Buffeting.]

defs: To strike with the hand or fist; to box; to beat; to cuff; to slap.
quotes: They spit in his face and buffeted him.

defs: To affect as with blows; to strike repeatedly; to strive with or contend against; as, to buffet the billows.
quotes: The sudden hurricane in thunder roars, Buffets the bark, and whirls it from the shores.
You are lucky fellows who can live in a dreamland of your own, instead of being buffeted about the world.

defs: To deaden the sound of (bells) by muffling the clapper.
To exercise or play at boxing; to strike; to smite; to strive; to contend.
quotes: If I might buffet for my love, or bound my horse for her favors, I could lay on like a butcher.

defs: To make one's way by blows or struggling.
quotes: Strove to buffet to land in vain.
